---
title: ""
---

::: {.hero}
# GLP-1R Expression and cAMP/PKA Signaling
**BNFO 420 Capstone — VCU**

Investigating how GLP-1 receptor activation affects downstream gene expression
across liver and fat tissue in obese mice using bulk RNA-seq analysis.
:::

::: {.stat-grid}
::: {.stat-box}
::: {.number}
60
:::
::: {.label}
Total Samples
:::
:::

::: {.stat-box}
::: {.number}
4
:::
::: {.label}
Genotypes
:::
:::

::: {.stat-box}
::: {.number}
14,152
:::
::: {.label}
Genes Analyzed
:::
:::

::: {.stat-box}
::: {.number}
4
:::
::: {.label}
Comparisons
:::
:::
:::

## What We Did

We analyzed a publicly available bulk RNA-seq dataset from mice treated with
mGIPR-Ab/P1, a dual-acting GLP-1R/GIPR agonist. Our goal was to test whether
GLP-1R activation upregulates downstream cAMP/PKA pathway genes across liver
and inguinal white adipose tissue (iWAT).

## Key Findings

::: {.finding-box}
**Drug effect was real but subtle** — treated and vehicle samples separated
clearly in PCA, but cAMP/PKA pathway genes did not reach strong significance.
:::

::: {.finding-box}
**Fat tissue responded more strongly than liver** — iWAT showed more
differentially expressed genes with treatment than liver did.
:::

::: {.finding-box}
**GIPR knockout had the largest effect** — unexpectedly, deleting GIPR from
CNS neurons caused more gene expression changes than either drug treatment
or GLP-1R deletion.
:::

## Navigate the Project

| Section | What you will find |
|---|---|
| [Introduction](introduction.qmd) | Background, hypothesis, and dataset overview |
| [Methods](proposal.qmd) | Pipeline, tools, and analysis approach |
| [Results](results.qmd) | All figures with interpretation |
| [Team](team.qmd) | Who we are and what we each contributed |
